sandbox._comment: Disabled: bwrap requires unprivileged user namespaces, which our seccomp filter blocks. The container itself is the security boundary. Sub-blocks are preserved inert so this file works unchanged if ever run outside macolima.

permissions._comment: Plan interactively (prompts on, you install deps), execute autonomously (tight denies, Anthropic-only egress). acceptEdits lets Claude freely edit code but still prompts for Bash not on the allow list. The REAL boundary is the egress proxy (domain allowlist) + seccomp (no user namespaces) + non-root + cap_drop; this deny list is defense-in-depth and surface-reducing, not the boundary itself. Claude Code's Bash matcher keys off the command prefix, so denies here can be routed around by wrapper commands (env, xargs, find -exec, make targets, language interpreters running local scripts). We deny the obvious routes; the proxy catches anything that makes it through.
permissions._webfetch_note: WebFetch is intentionally NOT on the default allow list. It runs server-side at Anthropic's infrastructure and bypasses the egress-proxy entirely — every URL the agent passes to it is a potential exfil channel (the destination server logs the URL, which can carry secrets in the path/query). Add per-project WebFetch allowlists to .claude/settings.local.json scoped to specific docs domains, e.g. `WebFetch(domain:docs.numer.ai)`. WebSearch stays on because results are summaries, not arbitrary URL fetches.
permissions._read_deny_note: The Read denies below cover the Read TOOL only. They do NOT block reading the same files via Bash (`cat`, `jq`, `python /tmp/x.py`, etc.) — those are allowed below for legitimate workflow reasons. The denies still narrow the most natural read path; treat them as nudges, not gates.
permissions._acceptEdits_note: acceptEdits skips the per-edit confirmation. Combined with the /workspace bind mount this means the agent can autonomously rewrite host-visible files. Do NOT run scripts, Makefiles, or `npm`/`make` targets from /Volumes/DataDrive/repo/<profile>/ on the HOST shell — only from inside the container — or you may execute agent-authored code outside the sandbox. To require per-edit confirmation, change defaultMode to "default".
